articles.title: Aluminium falls on EGA alumina restart, still set for weekly rise
articles.summary: Emirates Global Aluminium restarted alumina production at its Al Taweelah refinery in Abu Dhabi on 10 July, three and a half months after an Iranian drone attack shut the 2.8 Mt/yr facility; LME three-month aluminium fell 1.8% to $3,143.50/t on Friday as the restart signals that Gulf primary supply is returning, with EGA targeting 50% of refinery capacity within days.
articles.novelis_angle: Returning Gulf alumina output will progressively lift primary aluminium production back toward pre-incident levels, compressing the geopolitical premium that had supported LME pricing above $3,100/t; Novelis's metal input costs are indexed to LME cash, so sustained price retreat from mid-$3,100/t levels could benefit conversion margins if European ECDP premiums hold — the latter currently at $530/t, also down $8 on the week.
